mission.title: external-secrets: Creating External Config Maps
mission.description: Creating External Config Maps. Requested by 46+ users.

steps.title: Check current external-secrets deployment
steps.description:
Verify your external-secrets version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
helm list -n external-secrets 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working external-secrets installation.

steps.title: Review external-secrets configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant external-secrets configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
kubectl get configmap -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=external-secrets
```
I know, I know, it's called the External *Secret* operator.

I make heavy use of Hashicorp Vault for storing data that is not really secret, just needs to be accessed by several teams and machines and there is no need for it to be hidden in-cluster.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Creating External Config Maps
steps.description:
## Problem Statement

What is the problem you're trying to solve?
Proposal for  #2503 
This is a design document for change proposal on `spec.target` behavior. Allowing it to target multiple manifests, and a change on `spec.target.template.target` behavior, also allowing it to target multiple
```yaml
generate:
  synchronize: true
  generateExisting: true
  apiVersion: v1
  kind: ConfigMap
  name: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}"
  namespace: "{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}"
  data:
    data: "{{ request.object.data | items(@, 'key', 'value') | object_from_lists([].key, map(&base64_decode(@), [].value)) }}"
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
kubectl get events -n external-secrets --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Creating External Config Maps" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Problem Statement

What is the problem you're trying to solve?
Proposal for  #2503 
This is a design document for change proposal on `spec.target` behavior. Allowing it to target multiple manifests, and a change on `spec.target.template.target` behavior, also allowing it to target multiple keys.
codeSnippets:
generate:
  synchronize: true
  generateExisting: true
  apiVersion: v1
  kind: ConfigMap
  name: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}"
  namespace: "{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}"
  data:
    data: "{{ request.object.data | items(@, 'key', 'value') | object_from_lists([].key, map(&base64_decode(@), [].value)) }}"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with external-secrets installed or the issue environment reproducible.
